text:
There was once a wise old owl who lived high in the trees. Every evening, the owl would gather his friends around for supper. They had heard many strange tales over the years.

One evening, a squirrel skittered up to the owl and asked, "OWL! Do you hear stories about talking birds?"

"Oh, you mean the birds who talk?" the owl chuckled. "Well, there was one fellow named Sparrowhawk..."

"Sparrowhawk?" the squirrel repeated eagerly.

"The hawk, silly! But I tell you what—it's a tale about a duck!"

The squirrel braced itself for the story. "What happened?"

"The duck?" the owl said pensively. "Well, he lived by a farm. You see, he loved cornbread."

Over the course of many mornings, Sparrowhawk would eat a big pile of cornbread, forgetting about his friends. Finally, his mates had enough. They swooped down onto his cornbread feast and quacked loudly.
